evidences.title: SoftBank Group Corp Q3-2026 Earnings Call
evidences.content: ### AI Summary Earnings Call on Feb 12, 2026 Strong Net Income: Net income for the first nine months reached JPY 3.17 trillion, up JPY 2.54 trillion year-over-year, reflecting a significant improvement. Major AI Investments: Completed a $22.5 billion follow-on investment in OpenAI, bringing total investment to $34.6 billion and ownership to approximately 11%. Portfolio Shift: Over 60% of net asset value is now concentrated in AI-related investments, up from 18% three years ago. Arm Growth: Arm achieved record revenue, up 26% year-over-year, and issued a strong growth outlook that is above analyst consensus. Financial Discipline: Maintained loan-to-value ratio below 20% and cash position at JPY 3.8 trillion despite substantial investments. [...] Vision Fund Performance: Vision Fund 2 delivered positive gains, notably from OpenAI and other late-stage portfolio companies, while Vision Fund 1 saw some losses mainly due to Coupang. Digital Infrastructure Expansion: Announced acquisition of DigitalBridge for $3.1 billion to strengthen presence in digital infrastructure. evidences.title: SoftBank stock split: what’s next for 9984 shares? | Capital.com
evidences.content: The decision follows a period of strong price appreciation. On 10 November 2025, SoftBank’s shares reached approximately ¥22,255, about four times higher than their April 2025 low. Because the company trades in units of 100 shares, the rally pushed the minimum investment amount to more than ¥2,225,500 – a high entry point for many retail investors. Once the split takes effect, each existing share will convert into four new shares. The per-share price will be adjusted accordingly, reducing the minimum trading unit and widening access. SoftBank notes that the split won’t affect the total value of its common stock on its consolidated financial statements. ## Why did SoftBank conduct a share split? [...] Future portfolio adjustments, investment priorities or corporate actions will depend on SoftBank’s internal assessments and broader market conditions. This article doesn’t include analyst projections or external forecasts. ## Summary SoftBank has approved a 4-for-1 stock split effective 1 January 2026. The split follows a strong 2025 price rally, with shares reaching around ¥22,255 in November. The aim is to reduce the minimum investment unit and broaden accessibility. SoftBank has...

evidences.title: Does SoftBank’s 173% Surge Reflect Real Value After Fresh AI Investment Push?
evidences.content: Currently, SoftBank Group trades at a PE ratio of 20.8x. Compared to the industry average of 18.3x and a peer group average of 16.5x, the stock commands a noticeable premium. However, Simply Wall St calculates a Fair Ratio of 20.6x for SoftBank. This calculation brings together not only industry standards and peer comparisons but also considers company-specific factors such as earnings growth, profit margin, market cap, and risk profile. The Fair Ratio is more insightful than simple comparisons because it adapts to SoftBank's unique characteristics. In this case, with a current PE of 20.8x and a Fair Ratio of 20.6x, the gap is minimal and suggests SoftBank's valuation is very close to what is justified by its performance and future outlook. evidences.title: SoftBank Group Strategy and Business Model
evidences.content: Reorient the group toward AI. Management said in 2023 and 2024 that SoftBank Group was moving from a more defensive balance-sheet posture back toward selective offense, with AI as the central opportunity set. This is less a single project than a capital-allocation direction for the whole group. Build Arm as a core AI-era platform asset. Since Arm’s September 2023 initial public offering, SoftBank Group has been positioning Arm as a long-duration strategic holding. Public messaging has emphasized Arm’s role in data center, automotive, mobile, and edge computing, all of which benefit from rising AI workloads and power-efficiency requirements. [...] 1981: Masayoshi Son founded the company in Japan as a software distributor. 1994: The company went public, giving it access to public equity markets for future expansion. 1990s to early 2000s: SoftBank built a reputation as an internet investor, including its early involvement with Yahoo Japan and Alibaba. 2006: The acquisition of Vodafone Japan transformed SoftBank into a major telecom operator. 2013: SoftBank acquired Sprint in the United States, making a large bet on U.S. wireless. 2016: SoftBank acquired Arm, a landmark deal that la...
